config.title: Paris
config.coverNote: Four days, Right Bank base, the kind of Paris you do not get from a guidebook. I have your dinners locked, your museum sorted, and a short list of places I would actually send my own friends to. Everything below is yours; tap anything for the details. If a plan needs to move, I move it. Just text me.

days.title: Arrival, the Marais
days.summary: Land, settle in, easy first night close to the hotel. No alarms.

days.title: Left Bank day
days.summary: Pastry, the Orsay, and the dinner I am most excited about.

days.title: Your day, lightly held
days.summary: I left this open on purpose. A couple of ideas, nothing you have to do.

days.title: Departure
days.summary: One last croissant, then the car to CDG.

itinerary.title: JFK to CDG
itinerary.subtitle: Air France 23
itinerary.personalNote: Overnight flight, you land at 8:45. Sleep on the plane if you can; the day is easy on purpose.

itinerary.title: Airport car to the hotel
itinerary.subtitle: Private driver, meets you at arrivals
itinerary.personalNote: Karim will be past customs holding a sign with your name. He knows the hotel. If your bags are slow, text him; he waits.

itinerary.title: Hotel Bourg Tibourg
itinerary.subtitle: Marais, your base for the stay
itinerary.personalNote: Small, dark, romantic, exactly your speed. Room is held under my account; check-in is 3 but they will store your bags from the morning. I asked for the quiet courtyard side and they came through.

itinerary.title: Dinner, Le Petit Vendome
itinerary.subtitle: Easy first night, walk from the hotel
itinerary.personalNote: No reservation needed and that is the point on a jet-lagged night. Get the jambon-beurre at the bar; crispy baguette, good ham, butter, that is the whole trick. Glass of red, early night.

itinerary.title: Breakfast, La Maison d'Isabelle
itinerary.subtitle: The croissant stop
itinerary.personalNote: Won best butter croissant in Paris and it earns it. Go early, it gets a line. Eat it on the walk to the Orsay; it is fifteen minutes along the river.

itinerary.title: Musee d'Orsay, skip the line
itinerary.subtitle: Timed entry, I hold the tickets
itinerary.personalNote: Your entry is 10:30, go straight to the priority line and show the QR in your documents. Go up to the fifth floor first for the Impressionists before the rooms fill. The clock window is the photo everyone wants; it is on the way out.

itinerary.title: Dinner, Septime
itinerary.subtitle: The one I pulled strings for
itinerary.personalNote: This books out months ahead and your table is at 8:30 under my name. Do the tasting menu and let them pour the pairing; trust the room. Tell Claire I sent you. This is the splurge meal of the trip and it is worth it.

itinerary.title: Nightcap, Little Red Door
itinerary.subtitle: Cocktails after dinner, ten minutes from Septime
itinerary.personalNote: One of the best bars in the world and it does not act like it. No menu fuss, tell them what you are in the mood for. Walk here from Septime; it is a nice one back toward the Marais.

itinerary.title: Slow morning, no plans
itinerary.subtitle: On purpose
itinerary.personalNote: Best Paris days are the unplanned ones. Sleep in, get coffee, wander. The recommendations below are if you want them, not a checklist. If you decide you want a dinner tonight, text me by 4 and I will get you in somewhere good.

itinerary.title: Last croissant, Mamiche
itinerary.subtitle: Quick stop before the car
itinerary.personalNote: Modern bakery, the babka is the move if it is out. Grab a few for the plane. It is a small detour but worth it for the last taste of the trip.

itinerary.title: Car to CDG
itinerary.subtitle: Karim again
itinerary.personalNote: Karim picks you up at the hotel at 11:30, plenty of time for your afternoon flight. He tracks the traffic; if he wants to leave ten minutes early he has a reason.

picks.name: Le Bistrot Paul Bert
picks.subtitle: The bistro to know
picks.note: The definitive Paris bistro. Loud, bustling, and the steak frites is as good as it gets. If you want a second night out, this is where I would go. Tell me and I will get the table; they hold a few for me.

picks.name: Marche des Enfants Rouges
picks.subtitle: Lunch, market style
picks.note: Oldest covered market in Paris, five minutes from the hotel. Go hungry, walk a lap, then pick a stall. The Moroccan one is the one with the line for a reason. Good move on your open day.

picks.name: Shakespeare and Company
picks.subtitle: The bookshop, Left Bank
picks.note: Tourist famous, still worth it. Go upstairs where the reading room is and the crowds thin. Pair it with the croissant walk; it is around the corner from the river path to the Orsay.

picks.name: Candelaria
picks.subtitle: Hidden cocktail bar behind a taqueria
picks.note: Walk through the little taco place and the bar is through the door at the back. Order tacos first, then go through. In the Marais, close to the hotel, good late.

contacts.name: Gabe
contacts.note: Anything at all, any hour you are awake. If a plan needs to move, I move it.

contacts.name: Karim
contacts.note: Both airport transfers. Text him if your bags are slow.

contacts.name: Hotel Bourg Tibourg
contacts.note: Bag storage before check-in, concierge for anything in the Marais.

guideBlocks.heading: Your neighborhood, the Marais
guideBlocks.body: You are in the 4th, the heart of the Marais. It is walkable, full of small shops and good bars, and central enough that most of the trip is on foot. Head north for the quieter, cooler upper Marais; south for the river in ten minutes.

guideBlocks.heading: Getting around
guideBlocks.body: Walk first; this is a walking city and your week is built that way. For longer hops use the metro, it is fast and cheap. Skip taxis at rush hour, the metro will beat them. I have a driver on call for the airport runs and anything you do not want to figure out.

guideBlocks.heading: A few honest notes
guideBlocks.body: Lunch runs late and dinner later; 8:30 is normal. Say bonjour when you walk into a shop, it changes everything. Tap to pay works almost everywhere. Tip is already in the bill; round up if the meal was great.

documents.label: Air France boarding passes, JFK to CDG

essentials.label: Emergency
essentials.note: Works anywhere in Europe, English spoken. For medical only, 15 connects you to SAMU.

essentials.label: 24-hour pharmacy
essentials.note: Open around the clock. Closest late-night option to your side of the river.

essentials.label: Hospital
essentials.note: Central public hospital with an emergency department.

essentials.label: US Embassy
essentials.note: Lost passport or anything consular. Call before you go.

essentials.label: Me
essentials.note: Whatever it is, text me first. I have handled worse.
